refnamediv.refpurpose: Returns the distance between A and B trajectories at their closest point of approach.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: The |=| operator returns the 3D distance between two trajectories (See ). This is the same as but as an operator it can be used for doing nearest neighbor searches using an N-dimensional index.

note.para: This operand will make use of ND GiST indexes that may be available on the geometries. It is different from other operators that use spatial indexes in that the spatial index is only used when the operator is in the ORDER BY clause.
Index only kicks in if one of the geometries is a constant (not in a subquery/cte). e.g. 'SRID=3005;LINESTRINGM(0 0 0,0 0 1)'::geometry instead of a.geom

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.programlisting: -- Save a literal query trajectory in a psql variable... \set qt 'ST_AddMeasure(ST_MakeLine(ST_MakePointM(-350,300,0),ST_MakePointM(-410,490,0)),10,20)' -- Run the query ! SELECT track_id, dist FROM ( SELECT track_id, ST_DistanceCPA(tr,:qt) dist FROM trajectories ORDER BY tr |=| :qt LIMIT 5 ) foo; track_id dist ----------+------------------- 395 | 0.576496831518066 380 | 5.06797130410151 390 | 7.72262293958322 385 | 9.8004461358071 405 | 10.9534397988433 (5 rows)
